  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c1", "name": "Evidence-based policymaking", "aliases": ["Evidence-Based Policymaking"], "description": "The Act's organizing principle: developing and using evidence to support Federal policymaking.", "grounding_span": "Evidence-Based Policymaking Act of 2018", "location": "Sec. 1(a), 132 STAT. 5529"},
    {"id": "c2", "name": "Federal evidence-building activities", "aliases": ["evidence building"], "description": "Coordinated Federal activities to build and use evidence for programs, policies, and regulations.", "grounding_span": "FEDERAL EVIDENCE-BUILDING ACTIVITIES", "location": "Sec. 101, 132 STAT. 5529"},
    {"id": "c3", "name": "Evaluation", "aliases": ["program evaluation"], "description": "Systematic assessment of programs, policies, and organizations for effectiveness and efficiency.", "grounding_span": "the portfolio of evaluations, policy research, and ongoing evaluation", "location": "§313(d), 132 STAT. 5531"},
    {"id": "c4", "name": "Agency evidence-building plan", "aliases": ["learning agenda"], "description": "A systematic plan agencies must include in their strategic plan to identify and address policy-relevant questions.", "grounding_span": "Agency evidence-building plan", "location": "§312, 132 STAT. 5530"},
    {"id": "c5", "name": "Evaluation Officer", "aliases": [], "description": "A senior agency employee designated to assess and lead the agency's evaluation portfolio and policy.", "grounding_span": "a senior employee of the agency as the Evaluation Officer of the", "location": "§313, 132 STAT. 5531"},
    {"id": "c6", "name": "Statistical official", "aliases": ["statistical expertise"], "description": "An official designated to advise the agency on statistical policy, techniques, and procedures.", "grounding_span": "a statistical official to advise on statistical policy, techniques, and", "location": "§314, 132 STAT. 5531"},
    {"id": "c7", "name": "Interagency Council on Statistical Policy", "aliases": ["ICSP"], "description": "Council under section 3504(e)(8) of title 44 on which statistical officials serve.", "grounding_span": "the Interagency Council on Statistical", "location": "§314(b), 132 STAT. 5531"},
    {"id": "c8", "name": "Advisory Committee on Data for Evidence Building", "aliases": ["Advisory Committee"], "description": "Committee to review and recommend how to promote the use of Federal data for evidence building.", "grounding_span": "§ 315. Advisory Committee on Data for Evidence Building", "location": "§315, 132 STAT. 5531"},
    {"id": "c9", "name": "Chief Data Officer", "aliases": ["CDO"], "description": "A nonpolitical appointee designated in each agency responsible for lifecycle data management and data assets.", "grounding_span": "a nonpolitical appointee employee in the agency as the Chief Data", "location": "§3520, 132 STAT. 5541"},
    {"id": "c10", "name": "Chief Data Officer Council", "aliases": ["Council", "CDO Council"], "description": "Council in OMB establishing governmentwide best practices for data use, protection, and sharing.", "grounding_span": "Management and Budget a Chief Data Officer Council (in this", "location": "§3520A, 132 STAT. 5542"},
    {"id": "c11", "name": "OPEN Government Data", "aliases": ["open data", "Open, Public, Electronic, and Necessary Government Data Act"], "description": "Policy of making Federal data assets open by default.", "grounding_span": "OPEN GOVERNMENT DATA", "location": "Sec. 202, 132 STAT. 5534"},
    {"id": "c12", "name": "Open Government data asset", "aliases": [], "description": "A public data asset that is machine-readable, open-format, unencumbered, and based on an open standard.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘open Government data asset’ means a public", "location": "§3502(20), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c13", "name": "Open license", "aliases": [], "description": "A legal guarantee that a data asset is available at no cost and without use restrictions.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘open license’ means a legal guarantee that", "location": "§3502(21), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c14", "name": "Open format", "aliases": [], "description": "A format in which data assets must be made available.", "grounding_span": "make each data asset of the agency available", "location": "§3506(b)(6), 132 STAT. 5537"},
    {"id": "c15", "name": "Machine-readable data", "aliases": ["machine-readable"], "description": "Data in a format easily processed by a computer without human intervention while preserving semantic meaning.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘machine-readable’, when used with respect", "location": "§3502(18), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c16", "name": "Metadata", "aliases": [], "description": "Structural or descriptive information about data.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘metadata’ means structural or descriptive", "location": "§3502(19), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c17", "name": "Data asset", "aliases": [], "description": "A collection of data elements or data sets that may be grouped together.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘data asset’ means a collection of data ele-", "location": "§3502(17), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c18", "name": "Comprehensive data inventory", "aliases": [], "description": "An inventory accounting for all data assets created, collected, controlled, or maintained by an agency.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘comprehensive data inventory’ means the", "location": "§3502(15), 132 STAT. 5534"},
    {"id": "c19", "name": "Federal data catalogue", "aliases": [], "description": "Single public interface maintained by GSA for sharing agency data assets with the public.", "grounding_span": "which shall be known as the ‘Federal data catalogue’. The", "location": "§3511(c)(1), 132 STAT. 5540"},
    {"id": "c20", "name": "Public data asset", "aliases": [], "description": "A data asset maintained by the Federal Government that has been or may be released to the public.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘public data asset’ means a data asset,", "location": "§3502(22), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c21", "name": "Statistical agency or unit", "aliases": [], "description": "An executive-branch agency or unit whose activities are predominantly statistical.", "grounding_span": "agency or unit’ means an agency or organizational unit of", "location": "§3561(11), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c22", "name": "Statistical activities", "aliases": [], "description": "Collection, compilation, processing, or analysis of data to describe or estimate characteristics of groups.", "grounding_span": "means the collection, compilation, processing, or", "location": "§3561(10), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c23", "name": "Statistical purpose", "aliases": [], "description": "Description, estimation, or analysis of characteristics of groups without identifying individuals.", "grounding_span": "means the description, estimation, or analysis of", "location": "§3561(12), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c24", "name": "Nonstatistical purpose", "aliases": [], "description": "Use of data in identifiable form for any purpose that is not a statistical purpose.", "grounding_span": "means the use of data in identifiable form for", "location": "§3561(8), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c25", "name": "Confidential information protection", "aliases": ["confidentiality"], "description": "Protection ensuring statistical information under a pledge of confidentiality is used exclusively for statistical purposes.", "grounding_span": "§ 3572. Confidential information protection", "location": "§3572, 132 STAT. 5548"},
    {"id": "c26", "name": "Pledge of confidentiality", "aliases": [], "description": "An assurance under which respondents supply information exclusively for statistical purposes.", "grounding_span": "information acquired by an agency under a pledge of confidentiality", "location": "§3572(b), 132 STAT. 5549"},
    {"id": "c27", "name": "Identifiable form", "aliases": [], "description": "Any representation of information permitting the identity of a respondent to be reasonably inferred.", "grounding_span": "means any representation of information that permits the", "location": "§3561(7), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c28", "name": "Respondent", "aliases": [], "description": "A person or organization requested or required to supply information to an agency.", "grounding_span": "The term ‘respondent’ means a person", "location": "§3561(9), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c29", "name": "Designated Statistical Agencies", "aliases": ["Designated Statistical Agency"], "description": "Census Bureau, Bureau of Economic Analysis, and Bureau of Labor Statistics, authorized to share business data.", "grounding_span": "the term ‘Designated Statistical Agency’ means each of the", "location": "§3576(e), 132 STAT. 5552"},
    {"id": "c30", "name": "Data sharing", "aliases": [], "description": "Sharing of Federal data among agencies to build evidence.", "grounding_span": "how to facilitate data sharing, enable data linkage, and", "location": "§315(e)(2), 132 STAT. 5532"},
    {"id": "c31", "name": "Data linkage", "aliases": [], "description": "Linking of data assets to enable evidence building.", "grounding_span": "how to facilitate data sharing, enable data linkage, and", "location": "§315(e)(2), 132 STAT. 5532"},
    {"id": "c32", "name": "Privacy enhancing techniques", "aliases": [], "description": "Techniques recommended to protect privacy while enabling data use.", "grounding_span": "develop privacy enhancing techniques; and", "location": "§315(e)(2), 132 STAT. 5532"},
    {"id": "c33", "name": "Personally identifiable information", "aliases": ["PII"], "description": "Information whose disclosure poses privacy or confidentiality risk, individually or in combination.", "grounding_span": "of personally identifiable information, including the risk", "location": "§3504(b)(6)(A), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "c34", "name": "Access to data for evidence", "aliases": [], "description": "Mechanisms for statistical agencies and researchers to access data assets to develop evidence.", "grounding_span": "PART D—ACCESS TO DATA FOR EVIDENCE", "location": "Part D, 132 STAT. 5554"},
    {"id": "c35", "name": "Presumption of accessibility", "aliases": [], "description": "Presumption that agencies make data assets available to statistical agencies for developing evidence.", "grounding_span": "§ 3581. Presumption of accessibility for statistical agencies", "location": "§3581, 132 STAT. 5554"},
    {"id": "c36", "name": "Comprehensive risk assessment", "aliases": [], "description": "Assessment a statistical agency conducts on a data asset prior to public release.", "grounding_span": "conduct a comprehensive risk assessment of any data asset", "location": "§3582(b)(3), 132 STAT. 5555"},
    {"id": "c37", "name": "Sensitivity level", "aliases": [], "description": "Categorization of a data asset's sensitivity with a corresponding level of accessibility.", "grounding_span": "categorizing the sensitivity level of", "location": "§3582(b)(1), 132 STAT. 5554"},
    {"id": "c38", "name": "Standard application process", "aliases": [], "description": "A common process for applicants to request access to data assets for developing evidence.", "grounding_span": "establish a process through which agencies, the Congressional", "location": "§3583(a), 132 STAT. 5555"},
    {"id": "c39", "name": "Evidence", "aliases": [], "description": "Information produced as a result of statistical activities conducted for a statistical purpose.", "grounding_span": "The term ‘evidence’ means information", "location": "§3561(6), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "c40", "name": "Safeguard principles", "aliases": [], "description": "Principles Designated Statistical Agencies adhere to in protecting confidentiality of identifiable information.", "grounding_span": "safeguard principles, including—", "location": "§3576(b)(3), 132 STAT. 5551"},
    {"id": "c41", "name": "Lifecycle data management", "aliases": [], "description": "The Chief Data Officer's responsibility for managing data across its lifecycle.", "grounding_span": "be responsible for lifecycle data management", "location": "§3520(c)(1), 132 STAT. 5541"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d1", "term": "agency", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘agency’ means an agency referred to under section 901(b) of title 31.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘agency’ means an agency referred", "location": "§311(1), 132 STAT. 5530"},
    {"id": "d2", "term": "Director", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘Director’ means the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘Director’ means the Director", "location": "§311(2), 132 STAT. 5530"},
    {"id": "d3", "term": "evaluation", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘evaluation’ means an assessment using systematic data collection and analysis of one or more programs, policies, and organizations intended to assess their effectiveness and efficiency.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘evaluation’ means an assess-", "location": "§311(3), 132 STAT. 5530"},
    {"id": "d4", "term": "evidence", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘evidence’ has the meaning given that term in section 3561 of title 44.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘evidence’ has the meaning given", "location": "§311(4), 132 STAT. 5530"},
    {"id": "d5", "term": "State", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘State’ means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, each territory or possession of the United States, and each federally recognized governing body of any Indian Tribe, band, nation, pueblo, or other organized group or community which is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘State’ means each of the several", "location": "§311(5), 132 STAT. 5530"},
    {"id": "d6", "term": "comprehensive data inventory", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘comprehensive data inventory’ means the inventory created under section 3511(a), but does not include any underlying data asset listed on the inventory;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘comprehensive data inventory’ means the", "location": "§3502(15), 132 STAT. 5534"},
    {"id": "d7", "term": "data", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘data’ means recorded information, regardless of form or the media on which the data is recorded;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘data’ means recorded information, regard-", "location": "§3502(16), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d8", "term": "data asset", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘data asset’ means a collection of data elements or data sets that may be grouped together;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘data asset’ means a collection of data ele-", "location": "§3502(17), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d9", "term": "machine-readable", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘machine-readable’, when used with respect to data, means data in a format that can be easily processed by a computer without human intervention while ensuring no semantic meaning is lost;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘machine-readable’, when used with respect", "location": "§3502(18), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d10", "term": "metadata", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘metadata’ means structural or descriptive information about data such as content, format, source, rights, accuracy, provenance, frequency, periodicity, granularity, publisher or responsible party, contact information, method of collection, and other descriptions;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘metadata’ means structural or descriptive", "location": "§3502(19), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d11", "term": "open Government data asset", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘open Government data asset’ means a public data asset that is— (A) machine-readable; (B) available (or could be made available) in an open format; (C) not encumbered by restrictions, other than intellectual property rights, including under titles 17 and 35, that would impede the use or reuse of such asset; and (D) based on an underlying open standard that is maintained by a standards organization;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘open Government data asset’ means a public", "location": "§3502(20), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d12", "term": "open license", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘open license’ means a legal guarantee that a data asset is made available— (A) at no cost to the public; and (B) with no restrictions on copying, publishing, distributing, transmitting, citing, or adapting such asset;", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘open license’ means a legal guarantee that", "location": "§3502(21), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d13", "term": "public data asset", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘public data asset’ means a data asset, or part thereof, maintained by the Federal Government that has been, or may be, released to the public, including any data asset, or part thereof, subject to disclosure under section 552 of title 5; and", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘public data asset’ means a data asset,", "location": "§3502(22), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d14", "term": "statistical laws", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘statistical laws’ means subchapter III of this chapter and other laws pertaining to the protection of information collected for statistical purposes as designated by the Director.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘statistical laws’ means subchapter III of", "location": "§3502(23), 132 STAT. 5535"},
    {"id": "d15", "term": "agent", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘agent’ means an individual— (A)(i) who is an employee of a private organization or a researcher affiliated with an institution of higher learning (including a person granted special sworn status by the Bureau of the Census under section 23(c) of title 13), and with whom a contract or other agreement is executed, on a temporary basis, by an executive agency to perform exclusively statistical activities under the control and supervision of an officer or employee of that agency; ... and (B) who agrees in writing to comply with all provisions of law that affect information acquired by that agency.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘agent’ means an individual—", "location": "§3561(2), 132 STAT. 5544"},
    {"id": "d16", "term": "business data", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘business data’ means operating and financial data and information about businesses, tax-exempt organizations, and government entities.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘business data’ means oper-", "location": "§3561(3), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d17", "term": "evidence", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘evidence’ means information produced as a result of statistical activities conducted for a statistical purpose.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘evidence’ means information", "location": "§3561(6), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d18", "term": "identifiable form", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘identifiable form’ means any representation of information that permits the identity of the respondent to whom the information applies to be reasonably inferred by either direct or indirect means.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "means any representation of information that permits the", "location": "§3561(7), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d19", "term": "nonstatistical purpose", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘nonstatistical purpose’— (A) means the use of data in identifiable form for any purpose that is not a statistical purpose, including any administrative, regulatory, law enforcement, adjudicatory, or other purpose that affects the rights, privileges, or benefits of a particular identifiable respondent; and (B) includes the disclosure under section 552 of title 5 of data that are acquired for exclusively statistical purposes under a pledge of confidentiality.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "means the use of data in identifiable form for", "location": "§3561(8), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d20", "term": "respondent", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘respondent’ means a person who, or organization that, is requested or required to supply information to an agency, is the subject of information requested or required to be supplied to an agency, or provides that information to an agency.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘respondent’ means a person", "location": "§3561(9), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d21", "term": "statistical activities", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘statistical activities’— (A) means the collection, compilation, processing, or analysis of data for the purpose of describing or making estimates concerning the whole, or relevant groups or components within, the economy, society, or the natural environment; and (B) includes the development of methods or resources that support those activities, such as measurement methods, models, statistical classifications, or sampling frames.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "means the collection, compilation, processing, or", "location": "§3561(10), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d22", "term": "statistical agency or unit", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘statistical agency or unit’ means an agency or organizational unit of the executive branch whose activities are predominantly the collection, compilation, processing, or analysis of information for statistical purposes, as designated by the Director under section 3562.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the executive branch whose activities are predominantly the", "location": "§3561(11), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d23", "term": "statistical purpose", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘statistical purpose’— (A) means the description, estimation, or analysis of the characteristics of groups, without identifying the individuals or organizations that comprise such groups; and (B) includes the development, implementation, or maintenance of methods, technical or administrative procedures, or information resources that support the purposes described in subparagraph (A).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "means the description, estimation, or analysis of", "location": "§3561(12), 132 STAT. 5545"},
    {"id": "d24", "term": "accurate", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘accurate’, when used with respect to statistical activities, means statistics that consistently match the events and trends being measured.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘accurate’, when used with", "location": "§3563(d)(1), 132 STAT. 5547"},
    {"id": "d25", "term": "confidentiality", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘confidentiality’ means a quality or condition accorded to information as an obligation not to disclose that information to an unauthorized party.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘confidentiality’ means a", "location": "§3563(d)(2), 132 STAT. 5547"},
    {"id": "d26", "term": "objective", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘objective’, when used with respect to statistical activities, means accurate, clear, complete, and unbiased.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "means accurate, clear, complete,", "location": "§3563(d)(3), 132 STAT. 5547"},
    {"id": "d27", "term": "relevant", "verbatim_text": "The term ‘relevant’, when used with respect to statistical information, means processes, activities, and other such matters likely to be useful to policymakers and public and private sector data users.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "The term ‘relevant’, when used with", "location": "§3563(d)(4), 132 STAT. 5547"},
    {"id": "d28", "term": "Designated Statistical Agency", "verbatim_text": "the term ‘Designated Statistical Agency’ means each of the following: (1) The Census Bureau of the Department of Commerce. (2) The Bureau of Economic Analysis of the Department of Commerce. (3) The Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2019-01-14", "grounding_span": "the term ‘Designated Statistical Agency’ means each of the", "location": "§3576(e), 132 STAT. 5552"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "Individuals, businesses, and other organizations have varying degrees of legal protection when providing information to the agencies for strictly statistical purposes.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Individuals, businesses, and other organizations have", "location": "§3571(1), 132 STAT. 5548"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "Pledges of confidentiality by agencies provide assurances to the public that information provided for exclusively statistical purposes will be held in confidence and will not be used against such individuals or organizations in any agency action.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Pledges of confidentiality by agencies provide assur-", "location": "§3571(2), 132 STAT. 5548"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "Protecting the confidentiality interests of individuals or organizations who provide information under a pledge of confidentiality for Federal statistical programs serves both the interests of the public and the needs of society.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Protecting the confidentiality interests of individuals", "location": "§3571(3), 132 STAT. 5548"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "Declining trust of the public in the protection of information provided under a pledge of confidentiality adversely affects both the accuracy and completeness of statistical analyses.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Declining trust of the public in the protection of", "location": "§3571(4), 132 STAT. 5548"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "Ensuring that information provided under a pledge of confidentiality for statistical purposes receives protection is essential in continuing public cooperation in statistical programs.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Ensuring that information provided under a pledge", "location": "§3571(5), 132 STAT. 5548"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "Federal statistics are an important source of information for public and private decision-makers such as policymakers, consumers, businesses, investors, and workers.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Federal statistics are an important source of informa-", "location": "§3575(1), 132 STAT. 5549"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "Federal statistical agencies should continuously seek to improve their efficiency, but statutory constraints limit their ability to share data and thus achieve higher efficiency for Federal statistical programs.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Federal statistical agencies should continuously seek", "location": "§3575(2), 132 STAT. 5550"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "The quality of Federal statistics depends on the willingness of businesses to respond to statistical surveys; reducing reporting burdens will increase response rates and therefore lead to more accurate characterizations of the economy.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The quality of Federal statistics depends on the willing-", "location": "§3575(3), 132 STAT. 5550"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Enhanced sharing of business data among the Census Bureau, the Bureau of Economic Analysis, and the Bureau of Labor Statistics for exclusively statistical purposes will improve their ability to track more accurately the large and rapidly changing nature of United States business.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "Enhanced sharing of business data among the Bureau", "location": "§3575(4), 132 STAT. 5550"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "Congress enacted the International Investment and Trade in Services Survey Act (Public Law 94–472), which allowed the Census Bureau, the Bureau of Economic Analysis, and the Bureau of Labor Statistics to share data on foreign-owned companies.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Congress enacted the International Investment and", "location": "§3575(5), 132 STAT. 5550"},
    {"id": "cl11", "claim_text": "With part B of this subchapter, the sharing of business data among the three agencies continues to ensure the highest level of confidentiality for respondents to statistical surveys.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "With part B of this subchapter, the sharing of business", "location": "§3575(6), 132 STAT. 5550"}
  ],
